text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Monday, July 6, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** — 43 calls, 187 hands deep. Meade Electric is running **$53.80/hr** (10% above scale) on the massive Meta Data Center in Lebanon with 5-12s Monday-Friday and rotating Saturdays. Industrial Electric's Lilly LP1 new construction gig rotates 7-10s with a 13-day rule. This is the board to hit if you want consistent mega-project hours and rollups — book is moving steady.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** — 41 calls, 169 hands. Port Washington Data Center is reposting across MJ Electric, Gurtz, Cupertino, and Uihlein — all paying **JW wage plus $10/hr base** with ASAP starts. This is free money for anyone within striking distance. Multiple contractors means flexibility on crew placement.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** — 25 calls, 122 hands. Parsons Electric's Polaris Forge Data Center is the real prize: **$44.80/hr** scale, but **$100/day non-taxed per diem** Monday-Friday, bumping to **$150 Saturday-Sunday**, plus a **$100/day incentive** on top. Shift differential work available at 31.4%. This math beats most union halls on gross income if you can stomach North Dakota winters.

**LU-379 Charlotte, NC** — 17 calls, 106 hands. Gross Electric (MOC-1, Spartanburg) and Miller Electric (QTS, Rock Hill) are both offering **$4+ above scale** on data center work with 4-10s and optional Saturdays. Rosendin's Newton night shift throws in a **$150 boot voucher** after 90 days — every dollar counts.

**LU-683 Columbus, OH** — 23 calls, 117 hands. Mid City holds 25 calls at **$73.42/hr** across various locations; ESI's Cologix Data Center pays the same with all OT double time and locked Monday-Friday 40-hour guarantees.

**The data-center gold rush is real:** nearly half the board is saturated with semiconductor and server farm work, driving hands north and east while traditional industrial sits quiet.
